calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-25-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Melanie Emmons, appearing via Zoom as counsel for Petitioner Vanessa Bobb.

Ms. Emmons discusses tax liabilities discovered in October 2024 that required the amending of the petition and recalculating distributions. Counsel acknowledged that the distribution amount was incorrectly stated as $5,420.56 instead of $5,402.56, which she confirmed to be the correct amount.

Ms. Emmons further explains that Quan'Terrus Guidry has had multiple address changes within the same apartment complex. His current apartment was confirmed as #283. It was noted that no objections had been received, and heirs are awaiting payment. The court takes the matter under submission with intent to grant. The court requested updated pages 10-14 for the proposed order. Counsel stated she had requested that the revised pages be delivered via courier on Friday, November 22, 2024.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Deputy: Singh; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-24-2022 Continued To Nov-28-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kristina Lam, via Zoom, for Vanessa Bobb, Petitioner.

The court queries counsel regarding the efforts made to confirm the issue of the predeceased siblings. Counsel reports on the issue and points to a Declaration of Due Diligence Regarding Deceased Heirs that is pending in the court's e-filing queue which states that a paralegal at her firm was unable to locate any information regarding decedent's potential predeceased siblings, via heir-search websites.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to Nov-28-2022 to allow for the filing and court review of the Declaration mentioned above.

Judge: Hon. Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.
